[2018-01-24 15:39] steel0229e (Steel): while still getting elo farm from the rest
[2018-01-24 15:40] steel0229e (Steel): because they are still relatively much better better than the rest
[2018-01-24 15:40] mtanzer: mathematically speaking, top players leaving doesn't inflate ratings
[2018-01-24 15:40] mtanzer: there are other factors that might have done it though
[2018-01-24 15:41] steel0229e (Steel): is there not some distortion though?
[2018-01-24 15:41] steel0229e (Steel): if for simplicity we assume there are 2 groups of people: A (hardcore) and B (casual)
[2018-01-24 15:41] steel0229e (Steel): and we assume A always beats B
[2018-01-24 15:41] steel0229e (Steel): top of A leaves the game
[2018-01-24 15:42] mtanzer: can we instead say like A beats B 90%
[2018-01-24 15:42] steel0229e (Steel): so people were A- now become A
[2018-01-24 15:42] steel0229e (Steel): but A- loses some games to B
[2018-01-24 15:42] mtanzer: I don't get what you're saying here.  people who continue to play should have the same winrate vs. the same other people who continue to play
[2018-01-24 15:43] crash_overlord: Nah cause it wasnt just A players that left. B players left too
[2018-01-24 15:43] mtanzer: other people leaving doesn't change this
[2018-01-24 15:43] crash_overlord: so instead of having the top 100 be A>A->B>B-
[2018-01-24 15:43] crash_overlord: You now have the top 25 be A>A->B>B-
[2018-01-24 15:43] crash_overlord: The actual mmr doesnt change I mean
[2018-01-24 15:43] steel0229e (Steel): numbers don't but the average skill decreases
[2018-01-24 15:44] steel0229e (Steel): meaning same elo now means less skill
[2018-01-24 15:44] mtanzer: let me give the simplest example I can think of.  suppose there's only 3 players in the game: A,B,C.  A beats B 75% of the time, B beats C 75% of the time, and A beats C 90% of the time.  if A leaves, then B still beats C 75% of the time so nobody's elo will change
[2018-01-24 15:44] crash_overlord: Because the number stays the same
[2018-01-24 15:44] crash_overlord: so skill at the number is the same
[2018-01-24 15:44] crash_overlord: Average stays the same too
[2018-01-24 15:44] crash_overlord: assuming equal amounts leave everywhere
[2018-01-24 15:46] steel0229e (Steel): ok that's a good example mtanzer
[2018-01-24 15:47] crash_overlord: What you're mixing up steel is that you're saying that because #10 on ladder is now worse than #10 from a year ago, average skill has decreased. But that's wrong. Now instead of 100 people /average, you've got 25people/average. Which remains the same. And skill = elo. Raing on ladder = nothing. Playerbase size maybe.
[2018-01-24 15:47] mtanzer: also maybe worth noting that in my example, the average elo among active players does go down.  it's not like B and C's average rating becomes what the old average of A,B,C was before.
[2018-01-24 15:49] steel0229e (Steel): yes this is convincing
[2018-01-24 15:51] steel0229e (Steel): but if everyone except tier 9 players and me left the game, I'd be #1 and my ELO would be higher too
[2018-01-24 15:51] steel0229e (Steel): average skill would drop too
[2018-01-24 15:52] crash_overlord: Sure but you're assuming top players left and no others left
[2018-01-24 15:52] crash_overlord: which is just... wrong
[2018-01-24 15:52] crash_overlord: You just notice it more because you remember more names that were 1-20
[2018-01-24 15:52] crash_overlord: than the 60-100 ones
[2018-01-24 15:52] mtanzer: your elo wouldn't be higher because of that
[2018-01-24 15:53] mtanzer: if this actually made your elo higher it would only be because of a poor estimate in winrates for large rating gaps
[2018-01-24 15:54] steel0229e (Steel): thanks for the clarification guys
[2018-01-24 15:55] steel0229e (Steel): eloyot works differently than ELO, I don't know the details but it handles elo gaps differently afaik
[2018-01-24 15:56] steel0229e (Steel): without any knowledge of the system I can't really tell anything about relative ratings
[2018-01-24 15:56] mtanzer: I think lunarch has said before that the rating differences and winrates are somewhat similar to elo
[2018-01-24 15:57] crash_overlord: I think it's a modified trueskill
[2018-01-24 15:57] crash_overlord: or something like taht
[2018-01-24 15:57] steel0229e (Steel): Elyot blogged about the implemented change
[2018-01-24 15:58] steel0229e (Steel): losses and wins against much lower rated players result in less score change than in the ELO system
[2018-01-24 15:58] steel0229e (Steel): but before the change the system was already different

[2018-01-26 01:28] elyot: We're very conservative with new mechanics because we want to optimize the ratio of insightful strategic concepts to kolmogorov complexity, that's basically our #1 design goal along with maximizing the ratio of available viable builds/strategies to available cards
[2018-01-26 01:29] elyot: Chill was chosen because we found it had an unexpectedly high number of interesting consequences relative to its complexity
[2018-01-26 01:31] elyot: we almost merged lifespan and stamina into one mechanic, but we decided that they were different enough that we should include both, since chilling lifespan 1 units is too important to not have lifespan

[2018-01-29 02:27] 307th: so if they're getting really good investments, inflation is high, while if they're getting weaker investments, inflation is low
[2018-01-29 02:28] 307th: attackers are treated as an investment in prismata math, so actually, inflation typically gets higher as the game progresses
[2018-01-29 02:28] 307th: the idea is that, as players are having a harder time defending, they have to use more desperate defense - like, maybe early on they can defend everything just with Walls, but later they have to make Rhinos and even later they have to hold Drones
[2018-01-29 02:28] 307th: so, if you're buying an attacker in the lategame, it's getting insane value because it's killing Rhinos or Drones
[2018-01-29 02:28] 307th: so that means inflation's gone up
[2018-01-29 02:29] 307th: more fundamentally, attack has a higher ROI than economy
[2018-01-29 02:29] 307th: as long as the attack is doing damage
[2018-01-29 02:30] 307th: a simple example of this: say you buy a Tarsier. If your opponent wants to defend the Tarsiers with Engineers, they have to buy an Engineer every turn, starting on their next turn
[2018-01-29 02:30] 307th: so the Tarsier is basically -2 Gold/turn for your opponent for 4R
[2018-01-29 02:30] 307th: so, in the early game, where you can't make attack without running into absorb, inflation is relatively low
[2018-01-29 02:31] 307th: since the best investment is Drones
[2018-01-29 02:31] .jrkirby (jrkirby): So inflation is kinda like attack/gold ratio or something?
[2018-01-29 02:31] 307th: at this stage, inflation is somewhere between 1.28 and 1.333 (or, if they're using the half-turn inflation, 1.13 - 1.15)
[2018-01-29 02:32] 307th: no, extra attack doesn't always have much of an effect on inflation
[2018-01-29 02:32] 307th: a general rule of thumb: pre-absorb, inflation's around 1.3, post-absorb, it's around 1.4
[2018-01-29 02:32] 307th: and once shit starts hitting the fan, it can get way higher than 1.4
[2018-01-29 02:33] 307th: i'm planning on doing a post on inflation at some point but it's a ridiculously complicated concept that i don't fully understand myself
[2018-01-29 02:34] 307th: also it's not something you need to understand to be a good player
[2018-01-29 02:34] 307th: msven for example has said he doesn't bother with any of that math
[2018-01-29 02:35] ussgordoncaptain: Its really useful for knowing fi you should start building attackers turn 3 or turn 4
[2018-01-29 02:35] 307th: how does inflation help with that?
[2018-01-29 02:36] ussgordoncaptain: hmm it's actually harder to explain than I thought in words
[2018-01-29 02:36] ussgordoncaptain: It's easy to see graphically
[2018-01-29 02:37] ussgordoncaptain: But imagine comparing 2 peicewise functions
[2018-01-29 02:37] ussgordoncaptain: the other is x^1.5-10

[2018-01-29 02:38] 307th: i think understanding inflation can help, but understanding it intuitively can also work
[2018-01-29 02:39] 307th: kind of like, do you need to know calculus to play tennis
[2018-01-29 02:39] ussgordoncaptain: sure but the calculations give the intuition
[2018-01-29 02:39] ussgordoncaptain: Going "high means stop at 20 drones" is much easier with the calculation away from the board
[2018-01-29 02:40] 307th: yeah, i agree that learning about inflation can help get better at the game
[2018-01-29 02:40] 307th: just that there are other ways to gain the intuition
[2018-01-29 02:40] ussgordoncaptain: Some people get the intuition without doing calculations
[2018-01-29 02:41] ussgordoncaptain: but for those of us who aren't so smart (aka me) it is worthwhile to do the calcs to get the intuition
